name: wiki-leaning-meeting-extraction
goal: Make meeting extraction lean on the topic-wiki: inject existing topic-page content + topic-tagged L2 items so the LLM emits only deltas. Reshape recap into Core + Could include. Tag L2 entries with topics for precise per-topic queryability. Fix two latent bugs (L2 parser/writer mismatch, backend missing activeTopicSlugs) as side effects.

userStories.title: Add topics to L2 writer + MeetingMetadata
userStories.description: Extend MeetingMetadata with topics: string[], populate from frontmatter, write **Topics**: bullet alongside Date/Source in appendToMemoryFile. Add optional topics?: string[] to MemoryEntry/MemoryResult. Files: packages/core/src/integrations/staged-items.ts, packages/core/src/models/memory.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: MeetingMetadata includes topics: string[] (defaults to empty array)
appendToMemoryFile emits **Topics**: ... line when topics non-empty; omits cleanly when empty
Per-item entry header stays ## <Title>
MemoryEntry and MemoryResult extended with topics?: string[]
Unit test: writer produces correct entry shape with topics
Unit test: writer produces correct entry shape without topics (no **Topics**: line)

userStories.title: Memory parser update + getMemoryItemsForTopics helper
userStories.description: Extend parseMemorySections to single-pass classifier matching all three header shapes (## Title, ### YYYY-MM-DD: Title, ### Title) with anchored regex. Parse metadata bullets into structured fields. Add getMemoryItemsForTopics helper. File: packages/core/src/services/memory.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: All three header shapes parse correctly in mixed file
Header inside fenced code block does NOT match (anchored regex, code-fence aware)
Trailing whitespace tolerance on header line; empty title rejected
Metadata bullets parsed: topics?: string[], source?: string, date?: string
MemorySection extended with topics?: string[], source?: string
getMemoryItemsForTopics returns entries matching any requested slug, capped per-slug, recency-filtered (default 5/slug, 90 days)
Unit tests cover: three header shapes, code-fence negative case, empty topics filtering, recency window
Sanity check: parser run against worktree's actual .arete/memory/items/learnings.md and decisions.md produces sensible output (manual)
warnings: Pre-Mortem R1: Three-shape header match could double-match or mis-classify on real data. Use single-pass classifier with priority — each line classifies into exactly one shape; no fall-through. Sanity-check against actual learnings.md/decisions.md before merge.

userStories.title: New topic-detection.ts service (lexical pre-pass)
userStories.description: Pure function detectTopicsLexical(transcript, identities, options?). Tokenize transcript with normalizeForJaccard, score each identity (canonical+aliases) against transcript token set. Stop-token list for generic words. File: packages/core/src/services/topic-detection.ts (new).
acceptanceCriteria: Stop-token list as top-level const STOP_TOKENS = new Set([planning, review, sync, discussion, meeting, update, status, team, weekly, daily])
Threshold: ≥2 distinct multi-char non-stop slug tokens AND topic-token-coverage ≥ 0.5
Slug must hit on at least one non-stop token to score
Cap at 3 candidate slugs at rollout (Decision #6)
Sort by score desc; ties broken by last_refreshed desc
Pure function (no I/O); fully unit-testable
Unit tests: threshold cases (≥2 non-stop hits passes, single hit rejected)
Unit test: recency tiebreaker on equal scores
Unit test: stop-token rejection (weekly-sync slug NOT matching generic-status transcript)
Unit test: coincidence rejection (q2-planning NOT matching every transcript with planning)
warnings: Pre-Mortem R2: False positives silently suppress real deltas. Stop-tokens + ≥2 non-stop slug tokens + ≥0.5 coverage are the precision levers.

userStories.title: renderForExtractionContext helper on TopicPage model
userStories.description: Pure helper that concatenates Current state + Scope and behavior (truncated 1000 chars) + Open questions + Known gaps + last 3 Change log entries. Mirrors selectSectionsForBudget style. File: packages/core/src/models/topic-page.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders the four sections in stable order with clear separators
Scope and behavior truncated at 1000 chars when over
Last 3 Change log entries appended (configurable via opts.changeLogEntries)
Pure function; no I/O
Unit tests: full page rendering, missing-section handling, scope truncation, change-log limit

userStories.title: meeting-context.ts topicWikiContext enrichment + callsite sweep
userStories.description: Add topicWikiContext to MeetingContextBundle. Add topicMemory: TopicMemoryService to MeetingContextDeps. After related-context step, add Step 7 that runs detection, renders sections, fetches L2 items per topic. Sweep all callsites and mock factories. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-context.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: MeetingContextBundle.topicWikiContext populated when topics detected; undefined when none
MeetingContextDeps.topicMemory required
createServices() factory wires topicMemory if not already present
All callsite + factory mock updates committed (grep -r MeetingContextDeps and buildMeetingContext is clean)
Backward compat: existing tests pass with stub returning empty identities
Unit test: bundle has topicWikiContext.detectedTopics populated for meeting with known topic
Unit test: bundle has no topicWikiContext when transcript matches no topics
warnings: Pre-Mortem R5: Sweep all callsites and mock factories. grep for MeetingContextDeps and buildMeetingContext across packages/core/, packages/cli/, packages/apps/backend/, packages/core/test/. Update test factories to provide a topicMemory mock. Tests not exercising wiki injection use a stub returning empty identities.

userStories.title: buildTopicWikiContextSection + delta directive + char budget guard
userStories.description: Add wiki context section renderer. Wire into buildMeetingExtractionPrompt. Insert delta-only directive (full text in plan §Thread B) with When in doubt INCLUDE tiebreaker and one-shot CONFIRMATION-of-uncertainty example. Add MAX_TOPIC_WIKI_CONTEXT_CHARS=6000 with truncation order. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-extraction.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: buildTopicWikiContextSection renders wiki context as specified in plan §Thread B
Delta-only directive in prompt with all 5 DELTA rules + 4 do-NOT-emit rules
When in doubt, INCLUDE tiebreaker present verbatim
One-shot CONFIRMATION example (pricing $99/$149) present verbatim
MAX_TOPIC_WIKI_CONTEXT_CHARS = 6000 enforced
Truncation order: drop oldest L2 excerpts first; if still over, halve longest section; if still over, drop lowest-scored topic. Highest-scored topic never dropped
Pass activeTopicSlugs derived from topicWikiContext.detectedTopics[].slug to existing slug-bias system
Unit tests: prompt rendering with wiki context, prompt-char measurement, truncation behavior at each tier, 'when in doubt INCLUDE' string presence, CONFIRMATION example presence
warnings: Pre-Mortem R3: LLM over-suppression is highest-impact, invisible failure mode. The 'When in doubt, INCLUDE' tiebreaker + one-shot CONFIRMATION-of-uncertainty example are load-bearing — verify both appear verbatim in the assembled prompt.
Pre-Mortem R4: Token budget overflow. Implement truncation order exactly; instrument prompt-char totals.

userStories.title: core/could_include schema + parser + frontmatter sanitizer
userStories.description: Extend extraction JSON schema with core: string and could_include: string[]. Update parseMeetingExtractionResponse to parse both, validate could_include (trim, ≤200 chars, hard-cap 8), apply frontmatter-injection sanitizer rejecting line-start ---. Keep summary for backward compat. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-extraction.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON schema includes core (description: 'Free-form prose. Lead with most actionable, decided, or changed thing. Do not restate wiki content. No bullet caps')
JSON schema includes could_include (description with priority + 8-cap + headline-format guidance)
Parser hard-caps could_include at 8 entries (drops excess)
Parser trims items, rejects empty, rejects >200 char items
Sanitizer rejects raw --- in core and any could_include[] item — strips line, logs warning, does NOT fail extraction
summary parsing preserved (backward compat)
MeetingIntelligence extended with core?: string, could_include?: string[]
Unit tests: sanitizer (raw --- rejected with warning), 8-cap enforcement, trim/length validation, summary fallback when core absent
warnings: Pre-Mortem R7: LLM-generated strings flowing into staged sections of YAML-frontmattered meeting files must reject raw ---. Same sanitizer pattern in topic-page writes per topic-wiki-memory learnings (2026-04-23).

userStories.title: STAGED_HEADERS + formatStagedSections + formatFilteredStagedSections
userStories.description: Add 'core' and 'could include' to STAGED_HEADERS alongside existing 'summary' (DO NOT remove summary). Update formatters to render ## Core (with summary fallback) and ## Could include (omitted if empty). Files: packages/core/src/services/meeting-apply.ts, packages/core/src/services/meeting-processing.ts, packages/core/src/services/meeting-extraction.ts (formatStagedSections).
acceptanceCriteria: STAGED_HEADERS contains summary, core, could include (case as existing convention)
formatStagedSections emits ## Core (using core ?? summary) followed by ## Could include (only if list non-empty)
formatFilteredStagedSections accepts new params core and couldInclude; renders identically
## Could include block fully omitted when list empty (no empty header line)
Existing ## Summary blocks still parse without truncation in historical files
Unit tests: render with both fields, render with only core, render with summary fallback, render with empty could_include (block omitted)
Manual grep: grep -r '## Summary' packages/apps/ packages/cli/test/golden/ reviewed for downstream assumptions
warnings: Pre-Mortem R6: Per integrations LEARNINGS, parseStagedSections stops at any ## non-staged header — removing summary would silently truncate historical meeting files on re-parse. Grep ## Summary across packages/apps/ and packages/cli/test/golden/ before merging.

userStories.title: CLI --dry-run-topics flag
userStories.description: Add --dry-run-topics flag to arete meeting extract. Runs detectTopicsLexical, prints detected topics with scores + matching tokens, skips actual extraction. Used for empirical tuning. File: packages/cli/src/commands/meeting.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: --dry-run-topics flag accepted on arete meeting extract
When set: runs topic detection only, prints results, exits without writing extraction artifacts
Output includes per-detected-slug: score, matching tokens (separated into stop and non-stop), last_refreshed date
Works with --json for machine-readable output
CLI smoke test: invocation with sample transcript prints expected structure, exits cleanly
warnings: Pre-Mortem R2: This flag is the empirical-tuning lever. Output must include enough detail to debug false positives.

userStories.title: Call-site plumbing — CLI + backend (incl. activeTopicSlugs fix)
userStories.description: CLI meeting.ts:981 and backend agent.ts:220: thread intelligence.core ?? intelligence.summary and intelligence.could_include to formatFilteredStagedSections. Backend: ALSO pass activeTopicSlugs (latent gap fixed).
acceptanceCriteria: CLI extract command threads core + could_include through to formatter
Backend agent.ts threads same fields through to formatter
Backend agent.ts passes activeTopicSlugs to extraction (matches CLI behavior)
Existing CLI golden tests pass or are deliberately updated with rationale
Backend integration tests pass or are updated with rationale
warnings: Pre-Mortem R9: Backend activeTopicSlugs addition is a behavior change for the web path. Check fixtures that assume current (slug-bias-disabled) extraction output; update or note as expected drift.

userStories.title: 5-meeting A/B validation (merge gate)
userStories.description: Run extraction on 5 historical meetings with diverse topic coverage in two modes: Control (current main) vs Treatment (this branch). Compare item counts, save to ab-results.md (uncommitted). This task is the final pre-merge sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 historical meetings selected with diverse topic coverage (mix of fresh-topic and wiki-resident-topic meetings)
Both runs executed; results saved to dev/work/plans/wiki-leaning-meeting-extraction/ab-results.md
Acceptance: Treatment ≤ Control in item counts on most meetings (deltas only is the goal)
No meeting where Treatment loses an item that Control captured AND that the wiki didn't already record (manual inspection of borderlines, max 1-2 expected)
No fabricated items in Treatment that Control didn't surface
ab-results.md documents the comparison and verdict
This task is the final pre-merge sign-off
warnings: Pre-Mortem R3: LLM over-suppression is invisible. This A/B is the only signal we have before merge.
